claim: A new domain MUST be founded in wave order, not bottom-up: wave 0 (the skeleton -- manifest purpose/goals plus a Process node naming stages and roles plus Axes) MUST land BEFORE wave 1 (artifacts -- EntityTypes, each wired via Process.drives_entities); wave 1 MUST land BEFORE wave 2 (rules -- Requirements); wave 2 MUST land BEFORE wave 3 (tensions -- Assumptions/Conflicts); wave 3 MUST land BEFORE wave 4 (data -- EntityInstances). Each wave MUST close with a regeneration (`hotam gen-spec`) and a check of the overview projection (PIPELINE.md, R-domain-overview-projection) before the next wave opens. The diff against the domain's original source prose MUST run AFTER wave 0 (the skeleton), NEVER only at the end of the founding sequence.
why: Steward finding this session (task #203, dogfooded on the `prat` pilot domain, PRAT-hotam/domains/prat): the methodology already defines WHAT a domain needs (manifest, Process, Axes, EntityTypes, Requirements, Assumptions/Conflicts -- each with its own SETTLED requirements and check_* invariants) and HOW to add each node kind (the six-step mediation loop, R-... per node kind), but nowhere states in WHAT ORDER a brand-new domain should be founded. The `prat` pilot paid for this gap concretely: its Requirements were landed before any Process node existed (the Process-node-for-stages pattern only arrived later, task #199/#200, see R-process-aspect-first's successors and R-domain-overview-projection/task #201), and its manifest stayed empty for an extended stretch (partially fixed by task #200; full fix tracked as tasks #210/#211, not yet done at authorship time). The compounding failure this produced: an agent asked 'what are the stages of the PRAT methodology' could NOT answer from the graph + generated docs alone and had to fall back to reading the original prose document directly (diagnosed and partially closed by task #205 / R-domain-overview-projection, commit 7520545, docs/GEN-CODE-CONTRACT.md §7) -- a direct, observed consequence of building artifacts and rules before the skeleton (Process/manifest) existed to anchor them. This requirement is the general dogfood lesson generalized: it names the wave order (skeleton -> artifacts -> rules -> tensions -> data) that, had it been followed from day one, would have made R-domain-overview-projection's projection buildable and populated from the very first wave instead of retrofitted after 283 requirements had already accumulated with no Process anchor. `R-domain-overview-projection` (task #201, SETTLED/PROSE, feature-blocked on the PIPELINE.md generator) answers a DIFFERENT question -- 'how does a domain present its whole structure for READING once it has one' -- this requirement answers 'in what ORDER does a domain get WRITTEN in the first place'; the two are complementary halves of the same skeleton-first discipline, one about generation, one about founding sequence, hence the explicit `refines` relation below rather than treating them as duplicates. Correctly landed as PROSE/feature-blocked (R-speculative-aspects-frozen's sibling pattern, the same honest-roadmap-debt shape R-domain-overview-projection itself uses): no domain-founding-wave-sequencer tool exists yet to mechanically enforce wave ordering (e.g. refuse a ProposedRequirement land when the target domain has zero Process nodes and zero EntityTypes) -- the claim is real and SETTLED today as operator discipline (the six-step mediation loop enforces WHAT and HOW per proposal; this requirement adds the missing WHEN/ORDER dimension a human or agent founding a domain must follow by hand until the sequencer exists), the enforcer is honestly not-yet-built roadmap debt, not a silent omission.
evidence: the `prat` pilot domain (PRAT-hotam/domains/prat) landed Requirements before any Process node existed for its own staged methodology -- the Process-node-for-stages pattern only arrived later via tasks #199/#200
the `prat` pilot's manifest stayed empty for an extended stretch, partially fixed by task #200, full fix tracked as not-yet-executed follow-on tasks #210/#211

an agent asked 'what are the stages of the PRAT methodology' could not answer from the graph + generated docs alone, diagnosed as R-domain-overview-projection (task #205, commit 7520545, docs/GEN-CODE-CONTRACT.md §7)
